qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CSRF in Admin User Impersonation (Switch User)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets SwitchUserController CSRF in backend impersonation, not the recycler module hard-delete authorization path or missing table-modify checks described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Double Extension Bypass in File Upload
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets FileNameValidator upload extension handling and web-server PHP execution behavior, not recycler deletion of arbitrary TCA records without permissions.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: File Download Token Replay IDOR
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets FileDumpController token replay for file downloads, not RecyclerAjaxController/DataHandler hard deletion of TCA table records.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: IDOR in Resource/File Operations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets ResourceController file operations and file-mount authorization, while the advisory targets EXT:recycler deletion of database records from arbitrary TCA tables without tables_modify and recycler delete permission checks.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ImageMagick Command Injection via Malicious Filenames
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets ImageMagick filename/format-prefix handling in image processing, not recycler module access control for permanent record deletion.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Session Fixation - Missing Session Invalidation After Password Change
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets SetupModuleController session invalidation after password change, not recycler deletion authorization or DataHandler table-modify enforcement.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SVG Sanitization Bypass via Magic Byte Detection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type, CWE-79: Cross-site Scripting (XSS)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets uploaded SVG/MIME handling and stored XSS behavior, not recycler module hard deletion of arbitrary database records.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described EXT:recycler root cause: backend users with recycler access could hard-delete arbitrary TCA table records because recycler deletion and DataHandler hard-delete paths did not enforce the required recycler/UserTSconfig and tables_modify permission checks.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-p52w-7rhw-9m67 describes TYPO3 CMS recycler module broken access control allowing backend users with recycler access to delete arbitrary data from TCA-defined database tables.

Referenced TYPO3 security patch is titled '[SECURITY] Avoid record deletion without permissions in recycler module' and describes fixes in RecyclerAjaxController deleteRecords handling and DataHandler->deleteEl table-modify permission enforcement.

The seven final qualifying reports cover SwitchUserController CSRF, FileNameValidator double-extension upload handling, FileDumpController token replay, ResourceController file operations, ImageMagick filename processing, SetupModuleController session invalidation, and SVG upload sanitization.

None of the final qualifying reports mentions EXT:recycler, RecyclerAjaxController::deleteRecords, DataHandler->deleteEl hard-delete permission bypass, UserTSconfig mod.recycler.allowDelete, tables_modify checks, or arbitrary TCA database table record deletion.
